title: How to Automate Your Airbnb with Smart Home Tech

interlinking_suggestions.title: The Best Software for Managing Short-Term Rentals
The Best Smart Home Devices for an Airbnb

author.name: Marcus Thorne
author.bio: A former tech COO turned 'Business-of-One' consultant, Marcus is obsessed with efficiency. He writes about optimizing workflows, leveraging technology, and building resilient systems for solo entrepreneurs.

key_takeaways: Prioritize asset protection over guest convenience by installing automated guest-vetting software and insurance-compliant smart locks that provide a defensible audit trail.
Select your Property Management System (PMS) based on the power of its API integrations, not just its feature list, to create a resilient ecosystem of best-in-class tools.
Implement a strategic, automated guest communication funnel to proactively solve minor issues before they become negative reviews.
Use your PMS as a central dashboard to automate tasks for your local team, ensuring remote quality control and creating an unbroken chain of documentation.
Leverage your system's operational data (RevPAR, ADR) not just for daily management, but as a blueprint to drive your future property acquisition strategy.
summary: Professional Airbnb investors often mistake superficial gadgets for a real business strategy, leaving them exposed to critical risks and unable to scale effectively. The article advocates for a three-phase "Autopilot" framework that prioritizes securing the asset with professional-grade tools, then systematizes operations around a central Property Management System (PMS) to automate everything from guest communication to dynamic pricing. By implementing this system, operators can build a resilient, self-running business that delivers consistent five-star experiences, enables confident remote management, and provides the data-driven insights necessary for strategic portfolio growth.
ai_overview_answer: Professional Airbnb automation is a three-phase strategic framework designed to secure your asset, systematize operations, and prepare your business for scale. The foundational phase focuses on risk mitigation by implementing automated guest screening software, installing insurance-compliant smart locks with audit trails, and using privacy-safe noise sensors to prevent parties. Next, operations are systematized by selecting a Property Management System (PMS) with a robust API to act as the central brain for the business. This PMS then automates a strategic guest communication funnel and integrates with dynamic pricing tools to maximize revenue and ensure five-star reviews. For scaling, the PMS becomes a centralized dashboard to monitor key metrics like RevPAR and manage local teams through automated tasks. This data-driven system provides the insights needed to make informed decisions and confidently expand your portfolio.

itemListElement.name: How to Automate Your Airbnb with Smart Home Tech

@graph.headline: How to Automate Your Airbnb with Smart Home Tech
@graph.description: Ready to automate your Airbnb like a pro? Our guide details a 3-phase system for asset security, 5-star operations, and portfolio growth. Build your autopilot.
@graph.name: The Airbnb Autopilot: A 3-Phase Framework for Professional Automation

step.name: Phase 1: Secure Your Asset (The Risk Mitigation Layer)

itemListElement.name: Automate Guest Vetting, Not Just Your Welcome Message
itemListElement.text: The single greatest threat to your asset is a problematic guest, and reactive solutions are always too late. A professional system prevents high-risk individuals from ever stepping foot on your property. This is achieved by integrating dedicated guest screening software, such as Autohost or SUPERHOG, directly into your booking workflow. These powerful hosting tools are not simple ID checkers; they are comprehensive risk-assessment engines that verify identity, conduct background checks, and analyze risk signals. This proactive defense provides the documented, non-discriminatory evidence you need to cancel a suspicious booking without penalty, protecting your property, your neighbors, and your business reputation.

itemListElement.name: Install Insurance-Compliant Security, Not Consumer-Grade Toys
itemListElement.text: Your front door lock is more than a way to facilitate self check-in; it's a critical piece of legal and financial infrastructure. While many consumer-grade smart locks offer convenience, a professional operator must prioritize security that satisfies the stringent requirements of short-term rental insurance providers. You need a commercial-grade solution from a brand like Yale or Schlage. The crucial difference lies in the robust, timestamped audit trail. This digital log of every entry—recording which unique code was used and at what exact time—is an indisputable source of truth in a dispute. It can verify a guest's arrival, confirm a cleaner's entry, or disprove a false claim, transforming a potential liability into a defensible position backed by data.

itemListElement.name: Manage Compliance with Smart Sensors, Not Guesswork
itemListElement.text: Securing your asset means proactively managing compliance with local ordinances and HOA rules. Privacy-safe noise and occupancy monitoring devices, such as those from Minut or NoiseAware, are essential. These devices are built with guest privacy as a core feature. They do not record audio or conversations; they simply measure decibel levels and, in some cases, the number of nearby mobile devices to estimate occupancy. This provides objective proof to resolve noise issues before they escalate, helps adhere to regulations, and offers irrefutable evidence against false complaints.

step.name: Phase 2: Systematize Your Operation (The Five-Star Efficiency Engine)

itemListElement.name: Choose Your PMS Based on API Robustness, Not Just Features
itemListElement.text: The single most critical decision in your automation journey is your choice of PMS. The savvy professional prioritizes the quality and depth of its API (Application Programming Interface). Think of the API as the nervous system connecting your operational brain—the PMS—to its limbs: your smart lock, pricing tool, and cleaning software. Leading platforms like Guesty, Hostaway, and Hostfully are built with an integration-first philosophy. A PMS with a powerful, open API allows you to create an ecosystem of best-in-class tools that is far more powerful than any single, all-in-one solution, creating a truly resilient system with no single point of failure.

itemListElement.name: Implement a Strategic Guest Communication Funnel
itemListElement.text: Five-star reviews are manufactured through systematic, proactive guest communication. Your PMS is the perfect engine to automate this process. Design a strategic, multi-stage communication funnel: Booking Confirmation, Pre-Arrival Hype (3 Days Out), The "Soft Landing" (4 Hours After Arrival), Checkout Instructions (Evening Before Departure), and The Review Request (24 Hours After Departure). This ensures every guest feels attended to at critical moments and allows you to solve minor issues before they escalate into a bad review.

itemListElement.name: Leverage Dynamic Pricing as a Revenue Management Tool
itemListElement.text: A truly systematized operation uses data, not guesswork, to maximize revenue. Integrating a professional dynamic pricing tool like PriceLabs or Wheelhouse with your PMS transforms your pricing strategy from a static list of numbers into a responsive revenue management engine. These tools analyze hundreds of market data points to optimize your rates daily, allowing you to implement sophisticated strategies like orphan night premiums, far-future rate strategies, and occupancy-based adjustments that a manual approach could never replicate.

step.name: Phase 3: Scale Your Enterprise (The Remote CEO's Dashboard)

itemListElement.name: Unify your portfolio with a centralized dashboard
itemListElement.text: Your PMS is no longer just an operational tool; it is the single source of truth for your entire business. A CEO requires a clear, immediate view of performance, and a professional dashboard provides exactly that. Shift your focus to the key metrics that drive profitability: Occupancy Rate, Average Daily Rate (ADR), RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room), and Operational Costs. Watching these numbers allows you to make informed, high-level decisions, allocating resources to your highest-performing assets and identifying underperformers without getting lost in the day-to-day details of a single booking.

itemListElement.name: Systematize your local team management
itemListElement.text: Maintain rigorous quality control from a thousand miles away by using your PMS's task and maintenance modules to build a remotely manageable workflow. Instead of sending texts, you automate the entire process: a guest's check-out automatically triggers a cleaning task assigned to your primary cleaner, who receives a notification with all details and a unique access code. They use a dedicated software interface to view their schedule, access checklists, and report any issues with time-stamped photos, creating an unbroken chain of documentation.

itemListElement.name: Use your automation data to drive your acquisition strategy
itemListElement.text: After running this system for a year, your PMS contains an invaluable asset: a detailed performance blueprint for your specific market. You can analyze your historical pricing, occupancy, and review data to build a data-driven profile of a profitable property. You will know which neighborhoods generate the highest RevPAR, which bedroom count is most in-demand, and which amenities guests value most. These insights turn your operation into a growth engine, allowing you to confidently acquire new properties that fit your proven success model.

mainEntity.name: What are the biggest risks of automating an Airbnb?

acceptedAnswer.text: The primary risks are technology failure (e.g., a Wi-Fi outage or a dead smart lock battery), the loss of the human touch that can make an experience feel cold, and over-reliance on algorithms that might miss nuanced guest issues. A professional mitigation strategy anticipates these with redundancies, like a backup lockbox, and by designing an automated guest communication flow that feels authentic and caring.

mainEntity.name: How do you automate guest screening for Airbnb?

acceptedAnswer.text: You integrate specialized, third-party software like Autohost or SUPERHOG with your Property Management System (PMS). These platforms move far beyond basic verification. They use AI to run background checks, validate IDs against selfies, and flag high-risk patterns. This allows you to programmatically enforce your booking criteria and cancel problematic reservations before a guest ever arrives.

mainEntity.name: Can you run an Airbnb business 100% remotely?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but it requires a comprehensive "Autopilot System," not just a collection of apps. This system is built on three interconnected pillars: 1) Integrated Technology where a central PMS communicates flawlessly with your smart tech; 2) Automated Strategy for dynamic pricing and all guest communications; and 3) A System-Driven Local Team of cleaners and handymen who are managed through your PMS with automated tasks and digital checklists.

mainEntity.name: What is the best PMS for managing multiple properties?

acceptedAnswer.text: For operators managing five or more properties, enterprise-focused solutions like Guesty are often favored for their deep reporting and team management features. For professionals scaling from one to five properties, more agile platforms like Hostaway or Hostfully are excellent choices due to their powerful and flexible API integrations with other best-in-class tools.

mainEntity.name: Are noise monitoring devices legal in an Airbnb?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, in most jurisdictions, privacy-compliant noise monitoring devices are legal, provided two conditions are met: you must clearly disclose their presence in your listing description, and the devices must not record or transmit audio. Devices like Minut and NoiseAware are designed for this purpose; they only measure decibel levels, making them a privacy-safe way to prevent parties. However, you must always verify your specific local and state laws.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to fully automate an Airbnb?

acceptedAnswer.text: The investment has two parts. First, initial hardware costs for a professional-grade smart lock, a smart thermostat, and a noise sensor typically range from $500 to $1,000. Second, ongoing monthly software subscriptions for a quality PMS, a dynamic pricing tool, and a guest screening service can range from $50 to $200+ per property, depending on the platforms you choose and the scale of your operation.

mainEntity.name: How do smart home devices improve the guest experience?

acceptedAnswer.text: Beyond security, smart devices deliver convenience that guests appreciate. Seamless self check-in via a smart lock is consistently one of the most highly-rated features in reviews. A smart thermostat ensures the property is at a comfortable temperature upon arrival. Smart speakers can act as a digital concierge, instantly answering common questions like "What is the Wi-Fi password?" and adding a layer of effortless luxury to the stay.

body.content: The internet is saturated with advice on “automating your Airbnb.” Most of it is a superficial catalog of smart plugs and robot vacuums—tactics that mistake convenience for strategy. For the professional investor, this gadget-first approach is a distraction. Your goal isn’t to build a clever smart home; it’s to engineer a resilient, scalable, and profitable hospitality business that operates with precision, whether you’re across the street or across the globe.

This requires a shift in mindset from “host” to “operator.” It demands a strategic framework, not a shopping list. True automation isn’t about eliminating work; it’s about eliminating the *right* work, freeing you to focus on high-level growth. This is the blueprint for building your Airbnb Autopilot: a three-phase system designed to protect your asset, perfect your operations, and prepare your portfolio for scale.
Phase 1: Secure Your Asset (The Risk Mitigation Layer)

Our framework begins not with flashy guest-facing technology, but with a non-negotiable foundation: securing your asset. Before you can dream of optimizing revenue, you must systematically eliminate the catastrophic risks that keep professional operators awake at night. This foundational layer transforms your **Airbnb automation** from a mere convenience into an essential shield for your business, addressing the deep-seated anxieties around compliance, liability, and control that generic guides ignore. Think of this as building the digital fortress that allows you to operate with confidence.
Automate Guest Vetting, Not Just Your Welcome Message

The single greatest threat to your asset is a problematic guest, and reactive solutions are always too late. A professional system prevents high-risk individuals from ever stepping foot on your property. This is achieved by integrating dedicated guest screening software, such as **Autohost** or **SUPERHOG**, directly into your booking workflow. These powerful **hosting tools** are not simple ID checkers; they are comprehensive risk-assessment engines that:

items: **Verify Identity:** Validate government-issued IDs and use biometric checks to ensure the guest is who they claim to be.
**Conduct Background Checks:** Automatically screen guests against criminal and sex offender databases.
**Analyze Risk Signals:** Use AI to flag high-risk booking patterns, such as last-minute reservations from local guests, which are strongly correlated with unauthorized parties.

body.content: This proactive defense provides the documented, non-discriminatory evidence you need to cancel a suspicious booking without penalty, protecting your property, your neighbors, and your business reputation.
Install Insurance-Compliant Security, Not Consumer-Grade Toys

Your front door lock is more than a way to facilitate **self check-in**; it's a critical piece of legal and financial infrastructure. While many consumer-grade smart locks offer convenience, a professional operator must prioritize security that satisfies the stringent requirements of short-term rental insurance providers. You need a commercial-grade solution from a brand like **Yale** or **Schlage**.

The crucial difference lies in the **robust, timestamped audit trail**. This digital log of every entry—recording which unique code was used and at what exact time—is an indisputable source of truth in a dispute. It can verify a guest's arrival, confirm a cleaner's entry, or disprove a false claim, transforming a potential liability into a defensible position backed by data.

rows: **Access Codes**
Often requires manual updates; limited number of codes.
Integrates with booking platforms to auto-generate unique, time-limited codes for each guest and cleaner.
**Audit Trail**
Basic or non-existent entry logs.
Robust, timestamped audit trail of every entry, providing critical evidence for insurance claims.
**Durability & Security**
Lower security ratings (e.g., ANSI Grade 3).
Certified with higher security ratings (e.g., ANSI/BHMA Grade 2 or 1) and built with durable materials.
**Insurance Compliance**
May not be recognized by commercial underwriters.
Provides the verifiable access logs that insurers often require to successfully defend against liability claims.

body.content: Manage Compliance with Smart Sensors, Not Guesswork

Finally, securing your asset means proactively managing compliance with local ordinances and HOA rules. Privacy-safe noise and occupancy monitoring devices, such as those from **Minut** or **NoiseAware**, are essential. It is crucial to understand that these devices are built with guest privacy as a core feature. They **do not record audio or conversations**; they simply measure decibel levels and, in some cases, the number of nearby mobile devices to estimate occupancy.
Here’s how they protect you:

items: **Objective Proof:** If noise levels exceed a preset threshold for a specified duration, you receive an automated alert. This allows you to contact the guest and resolve the issue before it escalates into a neighbor complaint or a fine.
**Regulatory Adherence:** Many municipalities are now making these devices a mandatory requirement for short-term rental permits.
**Dispute Resolution:** The data logs provide irrefutable evidence to defend against false noise complaints from neighbors.

body.content: By layering automated vetting, insurance-compliant hardware, and privacy-safe monitoring, you build a resilient foundation. You are no longer just reacting to problems—you are systematically preventing them.
Phase 2: Systematize Your Operation (The Five-Star Efficiency Engine)

With your digital fortress in place, you can shift your focus from defense to offense—building the seamless operational system that consistently delivers five-star experiences and maximizes profitability. This is where you evolve from being a reactive landlord to a proactive hospitality operator. The goal is not just to save time, but to create a resilient, repeatable process that delights guests and runs on autopilot. A collection of disconnected apps won't get you there; you need an integrated framework where a central Property Management System (PMS) acts as the brain of your entire operation.
Choose Your PMS Based on API Robustness, Not Just Features

The single most critical decision in your automation journey is your choice of PMS. Many operators are lured by a long list of built-in features, but the savvy professional prioritizes the quality and depth of its API (Application Programming Interface). Think of the API as the nervous system connecting your operational brain—the PMS—to its limbs: your smart lock, pricing tool, and cleaning software.

Leading platforms like **Guesty**, **Hostaway**, and **Hostfully** are built with an integration-first philosophy. A PMS with a powerful, open API allows you to create an ecosystem of best-in-class tools that is far more powerful than any single, all-in-one solution. For example, when a guest checks out in your PMS, a robust integration automatically triggers a precise chain of events:

items: The guest's smart lock code is instantly deactivated.
A new, single-use, time-limited access code is generated for your cleaning crew.
Your cleaning management software is notified, and the turnover is automatically added to the schedule.

body.content: This creates a truly resilient system with no single point of failure, eliminating the manual work that leads to errors, delays, and negative guest experiences.
Implement a Strategic Guest Communication Funnel

Five-star reviews are not a matter of luck; they are manufactured through systematic, proactive **guest communication**. Your PMS is the perfect engine to automate this process, ensuring every guest feels attended to at critical moments. Move beyond basic "welcome" and "goodbye" messages and design a strategic, multi-stage communication funnel:

items: **Booking Confirmation:** Immediately after booking, send a message that confirms details and includes a link to your comprehensive digital guidebook. This empowers guests with information and reduces future questions.
**Pre-Arrival Hype (3 Days Out):** Send a message building anticipation, reminding them of key details, and providing clear instructions for a smooth **self check-in**.

**The "Soft Landing" (4 Hours After Arrival):** This is the most crucial, yet often missed, step. An automated message asking, "How is everything so far?" allows you to identify and solve minor issues (e.g., trouble with the Wi-Fi) before they escalate into a bad review.
**Checkout Instructions (Evening Before Departure):** A friendly reminder of the checkout time and procedures prevents confusion and ensures a smooth turnover for your cleaning crew.

**The Review Request (24 Hours After Departure):** A polite, automated request for a review—sent while the positive experience is still fresh—dramatically increases the likelihood of receiving five-star feedback.

body.content: Leverage Dynamic Pricing as a Revenue Management Tool

A truly systematized operation uses data, not guesswork, to maximize revenue. Integrating a professional dynamic pricing tool like **PriceLabs** or **Wheelhouse** with your PMS transforms your pricing strategy from a static list of numbers into a responsive revenue management engine. These tools do more than just raise prices on weekends; they analyze hundreds of market data points to optimize your rates daily. A professional operator uses them to implement sophisticated strategies that a manual approach could never replicate:

items: **Orphan Night Premiums:** Automatically increase the rate for single-night gaps between bookings, capturing value from travelers looking for a short stay.

**Far-Future Rate Strategy:** Set higher minimum stay requirements and premium rates for holidays and major events months in advance, securing high-value bookings before your competition adjusts their prices.
**Occupancy-Based Adjustments:** Create rules to automatically discount rates if the property remains unbooked close to the check-in date, prioritizing occupancy to avoid a total loss.

body.content: By integrating a powerful PMS, a strategic communication funnel, and a dynamic pricing engine, you build an operational machine that not only runs itself but consistently outperforms the competition.
Phase 3: Scale Your Enterprise (The Remote CEO's Dashboard)

With a perfected system for a single asset, you can now elevate your perspective from "host" to "portfolio manager." A truly automated system doesn't just run one property with flawless efficiency; it provides the data, control, and insight necessary to manage multiple properties from anywhere in the world. This is where you build your remote headquarters, with your PMS serving as the central dashboard for your entire enterprise.

Your first move is to **unify your portfolio with a centralized dashboard.** Your PMS is no longer just an operational tool; it is the single source of truth for your entire business. A CEO requires a clear, immediate view of performance, and a professional dashboard provides exactly that. Shift your focus to the key metrics that drive profitability:

rows: **Occupancy Rate**
The percentage of available nights that were booked.
Reveals demand patterns across different properties and seasons.
**Average Daily Rate (ADR)**
The average rental income per paid occupied night.
Tracks the pricing power and perceived value of each asset.
**RevPAR**
Revenue Per Available Room (ADR x Occupancy).
The ultimate indicator of your ability to fill properties at the best possible rate.
**Operational Costs**
Total cost per turnover (cleaning, maintenance).
Highlights efficiency and potential savings across the portfolio.

body.content: Watching these numbers allows you to make informed, high-level decisions, allocating resources to your highest-performing assets and identifying underperformers without getting lost in the day-to-day details of a single booking.

Next, you must **systematize your local team management.** This is how you maintain rigorous quality control from a thousand miles away. Use your PMS's task and maintenance modules to build a remotely manageable workflow. Instead of sending texts, you automate the entire process:

items: A guest's check-out automatically triggers a cleaning task assigned to your primary cleaner.
The cleaner receives a notification with the property address, time, and a unique, time-limited access code.
They use a dedicated software interface to view their schedule, access checklists, and report any issues with time-stamped photos.

body.content: This creates an unbroken chain of documentation. A damaged sofa is no longer a "he said, she said" phone call; it's a documented, time-stamped report with photographic evidence, logged in your central system.

Finally, you must **use your automation data to drive your acquisition strategy.** After running this system for a year, your PMS contains an invaluable asset: a detailed performance blueprint for your specific market. You can analyze your historical pricing, occupancy, and review data to build a data-driven profile of a profitable property. You will know which neighborhoods generate the highest RevPAR, which bedroom count is most in-demand, and which amenities guests value most. These insights turn your operation into a growth engine, allowing you to confidently acquire new properties that fit your proven success model.
Conclusion: You Are the CEO, Automation is Your COO

Turning operational data into an acquisition strategy is the moment you fully transition from owner to executive. The system you've built is no longer just a set of convenient tools; it is the operational engine of your enterprise. True **Airbnb automation** is a strategic decision to delegate relentless, day-to-day tasks to a system you control, freeing your time and mental energy for the high-level thinking that actually grows the business.

Think of yourself as the Chief Executive Officer of your portfolio. Your role is to set the vision, manage capital, and make strategic decisions about growth. The automation framework you've constructed is your Chief Operating Officer. Its job is to execute your vision flawlessly, manage daily operations, and ensure every detail runs according to the playbook you designed. You, the CEO, focus on *what* to do next—which market to enter, which property to acquire. Your system, the COO, handles the *how*—vetting the guest, managing the turnover, and collecting the data.
By building your business on this model, you methodically address the primary anxieties of a global professional.

items: You begin with a foundation of **risk mitigation**, using technology for robust asset protection.
You then **systematize your operations** for five-star efficiency, creating a predictable guest experience.
Finally, you **leverage the data** from this system to scale with precision, making informed decisions that reduce the financial risks of expansion.

body.content: This is how you create more than just a smart home. You build an "Airbnb Autopilot"—a resilient, profitable, and scalable asset designed to run without your constant intervention. It is an enterprise that provides you with the ultimate return on your investment: genuine peace of mind.
Frequently Asked Questions

items:
**What are the biggest risks of automating an Airbnb?**
The primary risks are **technology failure** (e.g., a Wi-Fi outage or a dead smart lock battery), the **loss of the human touch** that can make an experience feel cold, and **over-reliance on algorithms** that might miss nuanced guest issues. A professional mitigation strategy anticipates these with redundancies, like a backup lockbox, and by designing an automated **guest communication** flow that feels authentic and caring.

**How do you automate guest screening for Airbnb?**
You integrate specialized, third-party software like **Autohost** or **SUPERHOG** with your Property Management System (PMS). These platforms move far beyond basic verification. They use AI to run background checks, validate IDs against selfies, and flag high-risk patterns. This allows you to programmatically enforce your booking criteria and cancel problematic reservations before a guest ever arrives.

**Can you run an Airbnb business 100% remotely?**
Yes, but it requires a comprehensive "Autopilot System," not just a collection of apps. This system is built on three interconnected pillars: 1) **Integrated Technology** where a central PMS communicates flawlessly with your smart tech; 2) **Automated Strategy** for dynamic pricing and all guest communications; and 3) **A System-Driven Local Team** of cleaners and handymen who are managed *through* your PMS with automated tasks and digital checklists.

**What is the best PMS for managing multiple properties?**
For operators managing five or more properties, enterprise-focused solutions like **Guesty** are often favored for their deep reporting and team management features. For professionals scaling from one to five properties, more agile platforms like **Hostaway** or **Hostfully** are excellent choices due to their powerful and flexible API integrations with other best-in-class tools.

**Are noise monitoring devices legal in an Airbnb?**
Yes, in most jurisdictions, privacy-compliant noise monitoring devices are legal, provided two conditions are met: you must clearly disclose their presence in your listing description, and the devices must *not* record or transmit audio. Devices like **Minut** and **NoiseAware** are designed for this purpose; they only measure decibel levels, making them a privacy-safe way to prevent parties. However, you must always verify your specific local and state laws.

**How much does it cost to fully automate an Airbnb?**
The investment has two parts. First, initial hardware costs for a professional-grade smart lock, a smart thermostat, and a noise sensor typically range from **$500 to $1,000**. Second, ongoing monthly software subscriptions for a quality PMS, a dynamic pricing tool, and a guest screening service can range from **$50 to $200+ per property**, depending on the platforms you choose and the scale of your operation.

**How do smart home devices improve the guest experience?**
Beyond security, smart devices deliver convenience that guests appreciate. Seamless **self check-in** via a smart lock is consistently one of the most highly-rated features in reviews. A smart thermostat ensures the property is at a comfortable temperature upon arrival. Smart speakers can act as a digital concierge, instantly answering common questions like "What is the Wi-Fi password?" and adding a layer of effortless luxury to the stay.
